extra.reasoning: The property requires that flipping severe_headache from false to true (all else fixed) never lowers acuity. The enum order is [self_care, routine, urgent, emergent], low to high. Consider the witness baseline: chest_pain=true, palpitations=true, slurred_speech=true, severe_headache=false, others false. In this baseline, rule r5 (priority 100, sets self_care) does NOT fire because it requires severe_headache=true. The matching rules are r2 (chest_pain → urgent, priority 2), r7 (slurred_speech → urgent, priority 2), and r6 (palpitations → emergent, priority 3). The highest-priority match is r6, so acuity = emergent. Now flip severe_headache to true with everything else unchanged: r5's condition (chest_pain AND palpitations AND slurred_speech AND severe_headache) is now fully satisfied, and with priority 100 it dominates every other rule (the next-highest priority is only 3). Therefore acuity = self_care. Acuity drops from emergent (highest) to self_care (lowest), a strict decrease, violating monotonicity. The high-priority self_care override (r5) gated on severe_headache is precisely what breaks the 'setting severe_headache true never lowers acuity' intent.
